messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is essential to be aware of the potential dangers and take steps to protect yourself. Here are some tips: 1) Be cautious of sensational or unverifiable content, especially if it is popular or trending. 2) Verify the source of the information before sharing or believing it. 3) Use fact-checking websites or apps to check the accuracy of news and information. 4) Report fake accounts or content to the social media platform immediately. 5) Take breaks from social media and engage in offline activities to reduce exposure to potentially harmful content. By following these tips, you can protect yourself from the negative effects of fake content on social media."

response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is essential to be aware of the potential dangers and take steps to protect yourself. Here are some tips: 1) Be cautious of sensational or unverifiable content, especially if it is popular or trending. 2) Verify the source of the information before sharing or believing it. 3) Use fact-checking websites or apps to check the accuracy of news and information. 4) Report fake accounts or content to the social media platform immediately. 5) Take breaks from social media and engage in offline activities to reduce exposure to potentially harmful content. By following these tips, you can protect yourself from the negative effects of fake content on social media.

Here are some tips: 1) Be cautious of sensational or unverifiable content, especially if it is popular or trending. 2) Verify the source of the information before sharing or believing it. 3) Use fact-checking websites or apps to check the accuracy of news and information. 4) Report fake accounts or content to the social media platform immediately. 5) Take breaks from social media and engage in offline activities to reduce exposure to potentially harmful content.
